title: Mystacidium gracile
taxonomy: Mystacidium gracile is classified as an orchid. It belongs to the subfamily Orchidinae, specifically within the genus Mystacidium. The family to which Mystacidium gracile belongs is Orchidaceae. This classification places it among the terrestrial orchids found in tropical regions. As a member of Orchidaceae, Mystacidium gracile is further characterized by its unique growth habits and floral structures.

Mystacidium gracile has been used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world, particularly in Africa, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. The plant contains compounds that may help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Mystacidium gracile for medicinal purposes.
